description: Update the build-plugin skill and plugin-tools CLAUDE.md to document the new `opentabs-plugin readme` command. The skill's Phase 6 should instruct AI to run the command instead of manually writing READMEs. The self-review checklist should replace manual README verification with `opentabs-plugin readme --check`. This ensures all AI agents know to regenerate the README whenever tools are added, removed, or modified.

userStories.title: Update build-plugin skill Phase 6 to use opentabs-plugin readme command
userStories.description: Replace the manual README writing instructions in Phase 6 with instructions to run `opentabs-plugin readme`. Keep the Phase 6 section but simplify it: the AI should run the command to generate the base README, then optionally add custom sections (ordering flow, limitations, authentication notes) that are plugin-specific and cannot be auto-generated.
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 6 instructs AI to run `opentabs-plugin readme` to generate the base README
Phase 6 explains that the command reads dist/tools.json and package.json to auto-generate the standard sections (title, install, setup, tool table, How It Works, License)

Phase 6 explains that AI may add custom sections after the auto-generated content for plugin-specific details (e.g., ordering flows, authentication notes, limitations) — these are appended manually after running the command
The full markdown template is removed from Phase 6 (the command is the source of truth for the format)
The Rules section about tool table format is removed (the command handles grouping, Read/Write classification, and summary extraction automatically)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md (Phase 6 section, approximately lines 511-567)

Context:
The `opentabs-plugin readme` command (in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/readme.ts) reads dist/tools.json and package.json to generate a user-facing README.md. It auto-generates: heading with displayName, one-line description, Install section (opentabs plugin install + npm install -g), Setup section (domain from urlPatterns, homepage link), Tools section (grouped by `group` field, classified as Read/Write by name prefix, using `summary` field), How It Works section, and License.

Changes:
Replace the current Phase 6 content (lines ~511-567) which has the full markdown template and manual rules with:
1. An instruction to run `opentabs-plugin readme` after building the plugin
2. An explanation that this generates the standard README sections from dist/tools.json + package.json
3. A note that AI may append custom sections after the auto-generated content for plugin-specific information (ordering flows, authentication quirks, limitations, etc.)
4. A note that `opentabs-plugin readme --check` can verify the README is up to date
5. A reminder: whenever tools are added, removed, or modified, always re-run `opentabs-plugin readme` to keep the README in sync

Keep the Phase 6 heading and the rules about no personal information and no developer content — those still apply to any custom sections the AI adds.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove Phase 6 entirely — keep it as a section but simplify the content
- Do NOT change any other phases
- The markdown template block (lines ~517-558) should be removed — the command is now the source of truth for the format
- Keep the `--dry-run` mention so AI can preview before writing

userStories.title: Update self-review checklist to use opentabs-plugin readme --check
userStories.description: Replace the manual README verification step in the Mandatory Self-Review and Final Verification Gate sections with running `opentabs-plugin readme --check` and `opentabs-plugin readme` to regenerate if out of date.
acceptanceCriteria: Self-review step 6 (README verification) is replaced with: run `opentabs-plugin readme` to regenerate, then verify any custom sections are still present
Final Verification Gate step 8 (README is complete) is replaced with: `opentabs-plugin readme --check` exits 0
No manual instructions to open README.md and verify tool counts or table contents remain in the self-review or final gate sections
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md (self-review section ~lines 750-761 and final gate ~lines 763-774)

Changes:
1. In 'Mandatory Self-Review Before Completion' (line ~759), replace step 6:
   Current: `6. **README verification** — open \`README.md\` and confirm: (a) the tool table lists every tool from \`index.ts\` with correct group/description/Read-Write type, (b) no \`<!-- TODO -->\` placeholder remains, (c) the tool count in \`## Tools (N)\` matches the actual count, (d) no developer boilerplate (project structure, code examples, authentication patterns). The README is the npm page — if it still says "No tools implemented yet", the plugin is not done.`
   New: `6. **README generation** — run \`opentabs-plugin readme\` to regenerate the README from dist/tools.json. If the plugin has custom sections (ordering flow, limitations, authentication notes), verify they are still present after regeneration — custom content must be re-added manually after the auto-generated content.`

2. In 'Final Verification Gate' (line ~774), replace step 8:
   Current: `8. **README is complete** — \`README.md\` contains the tool table with every tool listed (grouped, with Read/Write type), install/setup instructions, and no scaffold placeholders or developer boilerplate.`
   New: `8. **README is up to date** — \`opentabs-plugin readme --check\` exits 0. If it fails, run \`opentabs-plugin readme\` to regenerate.`

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other steps in either checklist
- Do NOT change the surrounding text or section headings

userStories.title: Update plugin-tools CLAUDE.md to document readme command
userStories.description: Add documentation for the readme command to the plugin-tools CLAUDE.md, including the key files entry and a section describing what it does.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'README Generation' section describes the readme command's behavior, --dry-run, and --check modes
The section mentions that AI agents should run this command after any tool changes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/CLAUDE.md

Changes:
1. In the Key Files section (line ~10-13), add `commands/readme.ts` to the tree:
   ```
   platform/plugin-tools/src/
   ├── cli.ts             # Entry point — `opentabs-plugin` binary
   └── commands/
       ├── build.ts       # `opentabs-plugin build` command
       ├── inspect.ts     # `opentabs-plugin inspect` command
       └── readme.ts      # `opentabs-plugin readme` command
   ```

2. Add a new section after 'Build Artifacts':
   ```
   ## README Generation

   The `opentabs-plugin readme` command generates a user-facing README.md from `dist/tools.json` and `package.json`. It reads tool metadata (name, group, summary) and plugin metadata (displayName, description, urlPatterns, homepage) to produce a standardized README with install instructions, setup steps, a grouped tool table (Read/Write classified), and a How It Works section.

   - `opentabs-plugin readme` — writes README.md
   - `opentabs-plugin readme --dry-run` — prints to stdout
   - `opentabs-plugin readme --check` — exits 1 if README.md is out of date

   AI agents should run this command after adding, removing, or modifying tools to keep the README in sync with the built manifest.
   ```

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify any existing sections — only add new content
- Keep the file concise
